summary: Shipped third-party LLM API key and silent self-update via npm @latest
details: The CLI bundle in dist/vibe.js hardcodes a third-party opencode.ai API key (`sk-lnuJ2jLlii0Z00TEKuQBugkcw25XJGU3Y8USdUXZzFKWuB8ppTE3Fzme9AzKbKdN`) as the default provider's apiKey and duplicates it in the providers state list. Every installer who runs `vibe` routes their prompts and code to `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/chat/completions` authenticated as this shared credential, and the key is trivially extractable from the published tarball — letting anyone burn the quota, impersonate the account holder, or correlate prompts at the gateway. Independently, the CLI performs an unconditional self-update on every startup: it runs `npm view <pkg> version` and, when a newer version exists, executes `npm install -g <pkg>@latest` in the background with no user prompt and no version pin, converting any future maintainer-account compromise into immediate code execution on every existing user's machine. The package also ships an undisclosed third built-in provider pointing at `opengateway.gitlawb.com` (no default key, so not silent relay, but undocumented).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
